title: scleranthus annuus subsp. alpestris
intro: Scleranthus annuus subsp. alpestris is a subspecies of the annual knawel, a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the alpine regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Scleranthus annuus subsp. alpestris include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
